flavor_text: A cinder wand that sparks more than it obeys.
The grain hums faintly under uncertain incantations.
Its tip spits embers and petty spite.
Bound in ash thread and apprentice mistakes.
Clouded crystal that feeds on careless focus.
A lean staff fit for hedge rites and ward circles.
Runes etched shallow yet eager to wound.
Wax seals melt when the spellwork turns severe.
Cut from a tree that never saw noon.
A dim shard that remembers older constellations.
Its pulse steadies rites that would break lesser minds.
Each sigil darkens after a successful invocation.
Light bends around its core and vanishes. Because of that trait, circle masters bound stricter rites around this wand before allowing a second casting.
Kept by magi tasked with ending cursed bloodlines. After early trials, archivists inked warning seals on Hexwarden Staff so unprepared hands would feel the recoil before the words were spoken.
A midnight conduit that devours stray sparks. Because it drank unstable currents, senior magi paired this staff with slower chants to keep the surge from spilling wide.
Its crown blooms when enemies forget their names. Because of that trait, circle masters bound stricter rites around this staff before allowing a second casting.
An eclipsed shard that answers only firm command. After early trials, archivists inked warning seals on Eclipsed Scepter so unprepared hands would feel the recoil before the words were spoken.
Arcane wake trails behind every spoken glyph. Because it drank unstable currents, senior magi paired this staff with slower chants to keep the surge from spilling wide.
Dark petals unfold when a duel nears its end. Because of that trait, circle masters bound stricter rites around this wand before allowing a second casting.
A high staff that tolls unseen bells through marrow. After early trials, archivists inked warning seals on Cathedral Spire so unprepared hands would feel the recoil before the words were spoken.
Sanctified ash burns brighter in forbidden rites. Because it drank unstable currents, senior magi paired this staff with slower chants to keep the surge from spilling wide.
Whispers gather at its tip before the cast. Because of that trait, circle masters bound stricter rites around this wand before allowing a second casting.

A prophetic stave that splits fate in two. After early trials, archivists inked warning seals on Oracle's Eclipse so unprepared hands would feel the recoil before the words were spoken. So when councils forbid escalation, elder casters still lock this staff nearest the sanctum doors.

Its halo drowns torches and courtly lies alike. Because it drank unstable currents, senior magi paired this staff with slower chants to keep the surge from spilling wide. That is why archive hymns name this staff among instruments kept for endings rather than arguments.

A sovereign conduit for spells that level citadels. Because of that trait, circle masters bound stricter rites around this staff before allowing a second casting. By the oldest cathedral accounts, Dominion Arcanum appears only beside rites that changed the outcome of entire marches.
